INFO

Critical when outlier % exceeds the threshold.

INFO

INFO

► Setup ↑

Setup

Imports, theme, and connector wiring. Run this cell once when the kernel starts. Re-running is cheap — it just re-imports.

► Data Gathering ↑

Data Gathering

Pull a sample_df for column-level work, plus a per-day aggregate when a date column is available. Each query is guarded by FORCE_RELOAD — flip it to True in the Setup cell to re-query the warehouse on the next Run All.

► Univariate Analysis ↑

Univariate Analysis

► Flag & ordinal fields ↑

Flag & ordinal fields

Histograms for binary flags and low-cardinality ordinals (month, day-of-week, hour). label_threshold shows numeric labels above each bar when the panel has that many or fewer bars.

Histograms for Weather related fields. (rain, snow, freezing). label_threshold shows numeric labels above each bar when the panel has that many or fewer bars.

Histograms for Date related fields. (month, day-of-week, hour). label_threshold shows numeric labels above each bar when the panel has that many or fewer bars.

► Categorical columns ↑

Categorical columns

Low-cardinality categoricals (10): frequency + cumulative percentage.

High-cardinality categoricals (2): top-20 values per column with a null-rate strip.

► Count & aggregate metrics ↑

Count & aggregate metrics

Sum / mean / count totals for columns that look like aggregates (*_count, *_total, *_amount, *_revenue, …).

► Distributions ↑

Distributions

Showing first 12 of 15 continuous columns. Add more plot_distribution() calls as needed.

► Bivariate Analysis ↑

Bivariate Analysis

Pairwise relationships between continuous columns. The heatmap surfaces strong correlations at a glance; the scatter plots below pick the top pairs at runtime so they stay relevant if you re-sample sample_df.

► Correlation matrix ↑

Correlation matrix

Pearson correlation across all numeric columns in sample_df. Look for off-diagonal cells with |r| > 0.7 — those are columns that move together and may be candidates for feature reduction or hidden duplicates.

► Top scatter pairs ↑

Top scatter pairs

Computes |corr| at runtime, filters out pairs above 0.98 (likely derived columns) and below 0.10 (uninteresting), and emits up to 4 plot_scatter() calls in a loop.

► Temporal Analysis ↑

Temporal Analysis

Daily row count from PICKUP_DATETIME (already aggregated into daily_df by the Data Gathering section). Spot gaps, weekend/weekday cycles, and load-spike anomalies.
